mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative or zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns in more than 40 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding industrial production indicate synchronized strength in durable goods demand and construction activity, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, producing a cumulative information coefficient below 0.05 over three full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while core inflation simultaneously accelerates, real rates turn negative and trigger equity valuation compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60 percent of the periods where both series rise concurrently, invalidating the valuation compression premise.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The monthly change in INDPRO shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward equity returns across a 10-year rolling window (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real personal savings exceeding 2.0 percent forces households to draw down wealth buffers, signaling impending consumption weakness that reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Consumer spending remains resilient despite savings drawdowns, resulting in positive or neutral S&P 500 forward returns in over 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market recovery, which historically supports sustained equity risk premium expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The dual labor signal produces a negative or flat information ratio over two consecutive business cycles, indicating decoupling between employment growth and equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth paired with rising housing starts confirms a self-reinforcing demand recovery, which historically compresses credit spreads and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline or stagnate in more than 55 percent of instances where both GDP and housing data accelerate simultaneously, breaking the demand-recovery transmission channel.
